validation.summary: Help output documents quick/full modes, isolated LAUNCHDECK_HOME, JSON output, keep mode, and safety limits.
Windows-local quick smoke passed 11 real CLI steps: add, start, duplicate existing-run, status, task inspect, port inspect, restart, stop, reconcile, clean, and cleanup stop.
No listener remained on port 8888 and no matching demo smoke process remained.

controllerChecks: Smoke script runs the official demo from a temporary copy and never mutates the source demo project.
Smoke script uses an isolated LAUNCHDECK_HOME and only stops demo:dev through the Launchdeck CLI.
Smoke script does not use privileged hooks, process scanners, kill-by-port, or arbitrary PID termination.
Leader repair aligned duplicate-start validation with current Launchdeck semantics: a second start returns the existing run with process.spawned=false.
Leader repair excluded scratch directories from the temporary demo copy to avoid stale generated artifact contamination.
Evidence documentation keeps cross-platform-ready blocked until Windows, macOS, and Linux rows are all filled with passing smoke evidence.
acceptedResidualRisks: The executed smoke evidence is Windows-local only and does not satisfy macOS/Linux release evidence.
Full-mode release smoke remains owned by T037 after T036 package verification updates.
followUpWork: T036 update package syntax checks and full coverage commands.
T037 run quickstart/full release smoke and record final evidence.
